response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience and meaningful exposure to developer tooling, CI/CD pipelines, and testing infrastructure — but the role requires a people manager, and there is zero evidence of direct reports, team management, or organizational leadership anywhere in the resume. The minimum requirement of 2 years in people management is entirely unmet, and the 3-year technical leadership requirement is only partially satisfied through Staff-level IC roles rather than formal leadership positions. His technical breadth is impressive but insufficient to compensate for the absence of the core management competency this role demands.
strengths: Genuine developer tooling experience across CI/CD setup, testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP/agentic infrastructure at Google
Unusually broad technical stack spanning web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, and systems programming, demonstrating adaptability relevant to a cross-cutting productivity team
History of knowledge-sharing through workshops, open-source projects, and internal Refactoring Dojos, suggesting coaching instincts that could develop into management
concerns: No people management experience whatsoever — no direct reports, no hiring, no performance reviews, no org chart responsibility mentioned anywhere in a 17-year career
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or setting organizational priorities at any level; all roles are execution-focused IC positions
Developer tooling experience, while real, is fragmented and incidental rather than a sustained 3-year primary focus area; depth in build systems and test infrastructure is thin

key_factors.factor: No technical leadership or organizational change history
